body_txt: Need to stretch the story out a bit? Time to send The Hero on an errand! A Fetch Quest is a subquest of the overreaching plot which must be completed in order to trigger a vital Event Flag . Find a key, save a kid lost in the cave, defeat the monster attacking the town, rescue the trapped workers, resolve an Adventure Town's problem ... each of these is a Plot Coupon . Fetching back the coupons is name of the game. In most cases, Fetch Quests involve something minor and sidetracked, albeit, technically, even the main plot could be based upon fetching or retrieving a very relevant item (and be disguised so that your focus is elsewhere and you don't immediately notice). Games will often use a Broken Bridge to browbeat you into solving a Fetch Quest even if you should intuitively have more important things to do. This is also known as a FedEx quest, since they often consist of little more than receiving an object from an NPC and taking it to a particular person or place or going to a particular place and bringing back an object. The people, places, and objects themselves are largely inconsequential — you're just their mail carrier. While usually trivial, these quests can provide exposition through Show, Don't Tell , letting the player learn about the characters and their world by getting directly involved rather than through a monologue delivered by an NPC. They can also be used to introduce objectives outside of the main plot, making them useful building blocks for a Sidequest Sidestory . Plot coupons come in different shapes and sizes, and several variants deal with specific kinds: 20 Bear Asses is a variant to collect a given number of objects dropped by enemies. NPC Round-Up Mission is a variant to gather a group of missing characters. Herding Mission is a variant to corral a group of animals into a pen. Extend this quest type ad infinitum, and you get a Chain of Deals . If you have to fetch a lot of items but there's no in-universe character telling you to do it, it's probably a Collection Sidequest . If the fetched item is being stolen, you may be dealing with The Caper . Super-Trope to Product Delivery Ordeal . Compare You, Get Me Coffee .
